text:
There was a girl named Cathy. She lived alone, in a big house somewhere in the Midwest. She liked books—especially romance novels—and also liked to write her own stories.

One day, she wandered through her yard, lost in thought. She noticed something odd: a hole had appeared in a fence.

Curiosity got the better of her. Cathy slipped past the hole and into a small hidden world. There were flowers she'd never seen and creatures that scuttled among the grass.

She wandered along the edge of this magical place until she reached a small, lush pond. In the middle was a little boat. She climbed in but couldn't figure out how to propel it.

Just as she was about to give up hope, her eyes caught something blue. A fish? She reached out and... got caught. It was a lovely blue fish, gentle enough. 

It spoke.

"Cathy," it said. "Your heart is just as beautiful as your eyes."

Cathy blushed
